text:
Once upon a time, there was a frog who lived on an island. Every day, the frog would hop across the wetlands, searching for lenticels of bog butterworth. They had a magical property: every butterworth would sing a sweet lullaby that lulled frogs to sleep.

One frog, Olga, had a dream. She dreamed of finding a butterworth that sang a melody so profound it would carry her far away. After a long hop, she found one on a nearby tree. It sang:

"Sweet dreams, little frog, in a world of blue..."

Olga listened intently. Then with a leap she found her courage:

"I hear you!"

The tree swayed. "The night sky bleeds moonlight."

"I understand!"

The tree shook again. "Together, we'll cross oceans."

Olga was thrilled. She began hopping closer, mesmerized by the song.

On her ninth leap, a voice boomed from the branches. "Olga
